title: Research
description: Factual and analytical substrate under decisions — each entry captures what was investigated, how, what was found, what grounds the findings, and what stale conditions invalidate them.

schema_version.description: Semver of the block schema this envelope was last written against. Stamped by the write path to the schema's current version; when it differs from the installed schema's version, the read/write gates walk the block through the registered migration chain (or fail loud when no chain reaches the current version). Absent on blocks not yet written by a stamping write path.

status.description: planned = question framed, method chosen, no work started. in-progress = investigation underway. complete = findings_summary and citations finalized. stale = a stale_condition fired; findings may still be correct but grounding is no longer authoritative. superseded = a newer research entry replaces this one. revised = transient state during re-investigation against updated grounding.

layer.description: Which architecture layer the research informs. L1 identity/domain; L2 specification; L3 work; L4 execution; L5 memory.

type.description: Kind of research, per the research:* skill taxonomy. Affects reliability characteristics and staleness behavior.

method.description: How the investigation was conducted — e.g. 'code-inspection', 'empirical-test', 'web-fetch', 'curation of external document', or a combination.

scope.description: What was in scope — versions, branches, revisions, URLs, documents examined.

findings_summary.description: Self-contained, prompt-injectable summary of what was found. Target ~300-800 words. Must stand alone without the full findings_document.

findings_document.description: Path to the full rich document (typically in analysis/) for deep reads. May be null for research entries whose summary is sufficient or whose investigation has not yet produced a document.

dependencies.description: Version-pinned dependency strings (e.g. '@mariozechner/pi-ai@0.63.1') whose behavior is load-bearing for the findings.

revisions.description: Git SHAs, branch snapshots, or repo-at-date references.

external_refs.description: Dated external references — URLs with retrieval timestamps, academic papers, external spec documents.

grounded_at.description: Timestamp at which the grounding was current.

stale_conditions.description: Explicit list of conditions under which the research is no longer authoritative. A future staleness engine will read this to transition status from complete to stale automatically.

citations.description: Labeled source pointers grounding specific claims in the findings. Every factual claim in findings_summary should trace to a citation.

conducted_by.description: Alias for created_by; retained for research-specific provenance where 'conducted' reads more naturally than 'created'.

oid.description: Content-independent substrate-stable item identity (content-addressed substrate identity, Cycle 3). Minted once at item birth via mintOid(substrate_id); immutable across content versions. Optional in the schema so pre-Cycle-3 items validate; stamped on next write.

content_hash.description: SHA-256 (hex) of the RFC-8785-canonical content projection of this item (metadata fields excluded). Recomputed on every stamping write; moves with content, stable across metadata-only churn.

content_parent.description: content_hash of the immediately-prior version of this item; set on a content-changing update, absent on the first (v1) version. Forms the per-item content version chain.

transitions.note: Agent may mark own research complete when findings_summary and citations are finalized.
Any party marks stale when a stale_condition fires. A future staleness engine fires this automatically.
Reopening for revision; requires user authority if the research is currently cited by an enacted decision because revising may invalidate the decision's grounding.

transitions.precondition: superseding entry exists and points to this one via a research_supersedes_research edge

x-lifecycle.note: AJV draft-07 cannot enforce transition validity or required_authority; this block is metadata reserved for future framework-level state-machine validation and authorship attestation. A future staleness engine reads stale_conditions and fires the complete→stale transition automatically when conditions are met.
